title: Onoclea procera
intro: Onoclea procera, commonly known as the golden polypody, is a species of fern native to North America. The plant is rich in health benefits, particularly its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been traditionally used to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Onoclea procera include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant are teas and infusions, as well as tinctures and extracts, which are often used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
